task: BOUND-2: low-shell charge tau_0, tau_1 via a local-config automaton (finite check); confront circularity

single_level_b_L(r)_level_uniform.definition: b_L(r) = sup_q #{level-L stitch interiors in Cheb-r(q)} = shell-0 count t_0
single_level_b_L(r)_level_uniform.verdict: level-uniform (stable across 3 levels) -> tau_0=b(r), tau_1=b(4r/9), tau_2=b(4r/27)

per_shell_telescoped_sum_c(4.44).KEY_FINDING: per-shell decomposition tops out at ~18 > 12; shell maxima do NOT co-occur, so BOUND-2 (shell-by-shell) STRUCTURALLY cannot reach the availability threshold 12 -- that gap (18 vs true 12) is the anti-stacking loss

true_full_walk_c(4.44).note: the actual target quantity = 12 (<=12 with ZERO margin); achieved by an ARC-LIKE config

r=4.44.verdict: anchors in window = 5-7 (arc-like, 1-2 arcs), NOT the 216 cubic packing bound

r=10.verdict: nanch=13 at L8 EXCEEDS the naive linear per-arc bound 10 -> the reachable count is SUB-CUBIC but SUPER-LINEAR (fractal d~1.1); a single monotone arc can pack more than floor(D/3)+1 because it curves

finiteness_NON_circular.claim: the local-config alphabet is FINITE and level-independent
finiteness_NON_circular.proof: anchors A=M.p are Cheb-3-separated (min|M.v|_inf=3, exact) and M injective, so #anchors in Cheb-(r+4) <= P(r+4)=(floor(2(r+4)/3)+1)^3 -- a pure packing number, NO crowding hypothesis. Each interior is within Cheb-4 of its nearer anchor (finite-menu: reach<=4, interiors/word<=4, proven over 79M words). So the config (anchors + steps + connector words in the window) is drawn from a finite, level-uniform set.

rigorous_cubic_tau.tau_{>=3}(r): <= 1  (deep-tail collapse: min|M^3 v|_inf = 24 > 2r for r<=10, exact finite enumeration)
rigorous_cubic_tau.status: PROVEN, non-circular, FINITE -- but only reproduces the CUBIC no-blow-up bound (~1000), already known

circularity_verdict.finiteness: RESOLVED non-circularly (3-separation packing). The reachable-config SET is genuinely finite & level-independent.
circularity_verdict.tightness: CIRCULAR. The measured tight count (nanch 5-7, arcs<=2, sub-cubic ~D^1.1) is NOT forced by 3-separation (which only gives cubic). Proving the arc-like/sub-cubic count requires bounding RETURNS (#arcs) and anchor-density-along-the-reachable-path -- a property of the parent's LOCAL geometry = the SAME crowding quantity one level down. That is the circular dependency.
circularity_verdict.benign_via_contraction: The circularity is WELL-FOUNDED, not a true cycle: the config's parent-window is Cheb((r+4)*4/9), a radius that SHRINKS by 4/9<1 each level. So b_k(r) depends on parent crowding at a strictly smaller radius. The recursion reduces to the anisotropic contraction fixed point (phi=sigma_1(M^-1)sigma_2(M^-1)^{d-1}=0.353<1) already established -- the automaton INSTANTIATES that contraction, it does not give an independent finite escape from it.
circularity_verdict.reachable_config_count: grows ~linearly with walk length in raw coordinates (distinct anchor-arrangements: 3.3k/7.3k/15.7k at L6/L7/L8, r=4.44) -- does NOT saturate to a fixed finite automaton. But the BOUND-relevant invariants (max nanch<=7, max arcs<=2 at r=4.44) ARE level-stable.

bottom_line: The local-config automaton (a) rigorously & non-circularly reproduces the CUBIC boundedness (finite check via 3-separation packing), and (b) confirms the true availability-grade count c(4.44)=12<=12 is achieved by an arc-like config (<=7 anchors, <=2 arcs), level-stable. BUT the availability-grade tight bound is NOT a pure finite check: it needs the sub-cubic arc-density / bounded-returns lemma, which is circular and closes only via the proven 4/9 contraction. Moreover the per-shell (BOUND-2) decomposition structurally tops out at ~18 due to anti-stacking, so <=12 requires the DIRECT joint-config automaton, not the shell sum.
